problem.title: Get Target Using Number List And Arithmetic Operations
problem.description: Given a list of numbers and a target, find if you can reach the target using arithmetic operations.
assumptions: Return any valid expression, or empty string if impossible.

example.explanation: Insert +, -, or * between digits to reach target. Return expression if found.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Backtracking
solutions.description: Try inserting '+', '-', or '*' between every pair of adjacent digits using backtracking, propagating the running value and the last operand to correctly handle multiplication precedence. Leading-zero multi-digit numbers are pruned early to avoid invalid expressions. The key insight is that multiplication requires undoing the last addition or subtraction by tracking the previous operand separately.
